did_this_paper_use_ai_to_obtain_results_.details: The paper titled 'Multimodal skin lesion classification in dermoscopy and clinical images using a hierarchical attention fusion network' (Chunk 3-4) explicitly uses a novel hierarchical attention fusion network, a type of deep learning model based on convolutional neural networks (CNNs), to perform multimodal skin lesion classification. The method was validated on a dataset of 1907 sets of multimodal image pairs covering basal cell carcinoma, melanoma, nevus, squamous cell carcinoma, and seborrheic keratosis, achieving an average accuracy of 81.2%.

is_this_paper_a_review_paper_or_a_resear.details: The provided text contains multiple abstracts from the Journal of Investigative Dermatology (2021) that report original experimental and observational studies. Specifically, it includes a retrospective cross-sectional review of the National Health Interview Survey (NHIS) analyzing health information technology utilization (Chunk 1-2), a study on occupational exposures in systemic sclerosis using logistic regression on patient cohort data (Chunk 3-4), a technical study proposing a novel hierarchical attention fusion network for skin lesion classification validated on a constructed dataset of 1907 image pairs (Chunk 3-4), a statistical study of adverse drug reactions in melanoma patients treated with biological drugs (Chunk 5-6), and a prognostic study using 31-gene expression profiling on stage I cutaneous melanoma tumors (Chunk 5-6). These are all primary research articles, not reviews.

title: Health information technology utilization among skin cancer patients
brief_summary: This study utilized a retrospective cross-sectional review of the National Health Interview Survey (NHIS) from 2011 to 2018 to analyze health information technology (HIT) utilization among skin cancer patients. The primary outcome was whether patients scheduled healthcare appointments online, with secondary outcomes including looking up health information online, communicating via email, and filling prescriptions electronically. The analysis employed multivariable logistic regression to assess associations between sociodemographic characteristics and HIT utilization. Results indicated that the proportion of patients scheduling appointments online increased from 4.36% to 21.35%, while online health information lookup, email communication, and electronic prescription filling also rose significantly. Uninsured patients and those with lower income or education levels were significantly less likely to utilize these HIT services. The study concludes that substantial sociodemographic disparities exist in HIT utilization, suggesting that targeted interventions are needed to reduce health disparities among disadvantaged skin cancer patient groups.

lesion_types_studied.details: Studied in the context of patient attitudes towards active surveillance (Chunk 1-2) and as a class in the multimodal skin lesion classification dataset (Chunk 3-4).

all_task_types.target: Multimodal skin lesion classes (basal cell carcinoma, melanoma, nevus, squamous cell carcinoma, and seborrheic keratosis)
all_task_types.details: The paper proposes a hierarchical attention fusion network for multimodal skin lesion classification using dermoscopy and clinical images. The model predicts discrete diagnostic categories for five common skin diseases, achieving an average accuracy of 81.2% on a dataset of 1907 image pairs.

datasets_used.name: National Health Interview Survey (NHIS)
datasets_used.description: A retrospective cross-sectional review dataset from 2011-2018 used to analyze health information technology (HIT) utilization among patients reporting a skin cancer diagnosis.
classes_used: HIT utilization outcomes (scheduled appointments online, looked up health information online, communicated via email, filled prescriptions electronically)

use.other: Used for retrospective cross-sectional analysis and multivariable logistic regression to assess associations between sociodemographic characteristics and HIT utilization.

datasets_used.name: Canadian Scleroderma Research Group cohort
datasets_used.description: A cohort dataset extracted from the Canadian Scleroderma Research Group over the years 2003-2019, containing data on 1525 patients with systemic sclerosis (SSc), including gender, occupational exposure history, symptom severity, antibody profile, and mortality.
classes_used: Occupational exposure types (organic solvents, industrial fumes, silica, heavy metals, asbestos, epoxy resins)
SSc disease manifestations (diffuse SSc, renal disease, interstitial lung disease, mortality)

use.other: Used to compare SSc disease features for different occupational exposures and determine clinical characteristics associated with each exposure using logistic regression models.

datasets_used.name: Constructed multimodal skin lesion dataset
datasets_used.description: A constructed dataset of 1907 sets of multimodal image pairs (dermoscopy and clinical images) collected from Xiangya Hospital of Central South University over the recent 10 years, covering five common skin diseases.

use.training: Used to validate the effectiveness of a novel hierarchical attention fusion network for multimodal skin lesion classification.
use.testing: Used to evaluate the model's performance, achieving an average accuracy of 81.2%.

datasets_used.name: Melanoma biological drug treatment cohort
datasets_used.description: A study population of 263 patients with melanoma treated with biological drugs (Nivolumab and Pembrolizumab) from January 2019 to October 2020, analyzed for adverse drug reactions (ADRs) via medical records and the Italian National Pharmacovigilance Network.

use.other: Used for statistical analysis of adverse drug reactions, comparing ADRs between Nivolumab and Pembrolizumab treatments.

datasets_used.name: Stage I cutaneous melanoma 31-GEP cohort
datasets_used.description: A cohort of 850 patients with stage I cutaneous melanoma (CM) who underwent the 31-GEP test on primary tumors, with a subset of 325 patients receiving a negative sentinel lymph node biopsy (SLNBx) result.

use.other: Used to assess 5-year recurrence-free survival (RFS) using Kaplan-Meier and Cox proportional hazards analyses to stratify metastatic risk.

datasets_used.name: BCC Active Surveillance Survey Cohort
datasets_used.description: A pre/post survey study cohort of 203 patients in the dermatology clinic at the Minneapolis VA Medical Center from August 2019 to October 2020, evaluating attitudes towards active surveillance for basal cell carcinoma (BCC) before and after viewing an educational video.

use.other: Used to determine patients' attitudes and concerns regarding BCC active surveillance and evaluate the effect of an educational video.

statistical_methods_used.purpose: To analyze associations between sociodemographic characteristics and health information technology (HIT) utilization among skin cancer patients.
To determine clinical characteristics associated with occupational exposures in systemic sclerosis patients.

To quantify the association between specific occupational exposures (e.g., silica, organic solvents, asbestos) and disease manifestations (e.g., diffuse SSc, mortality, renal disease) in systemic sclerosis patients.
To assess 5-year recurrence-free survival (RFS) in patients with stage I cutaneous melanoma stratified by 31-gene expression profiling (GEP) results.
To assess 5-year recurrence-free survival and identify independent predictors of recurrence in stage I cutaneous melanoma patients.
To report the independent predictive value of Class 2B GEP result for recurrence in stage I cutaneous melanoma.
To evaluate the diagnostic performance of the 31-GEP test and sentinel lymph node biopsy (SLNBx) for predicting recurrence in stage I cutaneous melanoma.

statistical_methods_used.method: Chi-square test or similar significance testing (implied by p-values)
statistical_methods_used.purpose: To compare proportions of patients with concerns regarding basal cell carcinoma active surveillance pre- and post-video education, and to assess differences in HIT utilization across sociodemographic groups.

hypothesis_testing.test: Multivariable logistic regression (for HIT utilization study), Chi-square tests or similar significance tests (for BCC active surveillance survey), Cox proportional hazards analysis (for melanoma recurrence study)
hypothesis_testing.null_hypothesis: There is no association between sociodemographic characteristics (e.g., insurance status, income, education) and health information technology utilization among skin cancer patients; there is no difference in patient concerns regarding basal cell carcinoma active surveillance before and after educational video intervention; Class 2B 31-GEP result is not an independent predictor of recurrence in stage I cutaneous melanoma.
hypothesis_testing.alternative_hypothesis: There is a significant association between sociodemographic characteristics and health information technology utilization; there is a significant difference in patient concerns regarding basal cell carcinoma active surveillance before and after educational video intervention; Class 2B 31-GEP result is an independent predictor of recurrence in stage I cutaneous melanoma.
hypothesis_testing.interpretation: Significance was interpreted as p < 0.05 for the HIT utilization study, indicating substantial differences in HIT utilization across sociodemographic lines. For the BCC active surveillance study, p-values were reported for specific comparisons (e.g., p=0.0065 for concern about doctor monitoring, p<0.0001 for concern about frequent doctor visits, p=0.0070 for concern about making the wrong decision), with no significant difference found for comfort level (p=0.5517). For the melanoma recurrence study, p<0.001 was reported for the difference in 5-year recurrence-free survival between Class 1A and Class 2B groups and for the hazard ratio of Class 2B result.

National Health Interview Survey (NHIS).metric: Proportion of patients utilizing Health Information Technology (HIT)

Multivariable Logistic Regression.uninsured_association: Less likely to schedule appointments online, communicate via email, or fill prescriptions electronically (p<0.05 for all)
Multivariable Logistic Regression.low_income_association: Patients with income below 200% of federal poverty level less likely to look up health info, communicate via email, or fill prescriptions electronically (p<0.05 for all)
Multivariable Logistic Regression.low_education_association: Patients without a bachelor’s degree less likely to look up health info, communicate via email, or fill prescriptions electronically (p<0.05 for all)

National Health Interview Survey (NHIS).notes: Significant sociodemographic disparities in HIT utilization were observed. Uninsured patients and those with lower income or education levels were significantly less likely to utilize HIT services.

BCC Active Surveillance Survey Cohort.metric: Patient attitudes and concerns regarding active surveillance for Basal Cell Carcinoma (BCC)
BCC Active Surveillance Survey Cohort.notes: Significantly fewer respondents were concerned with their doctor monitoring their BCC post-video compared to pre-video. No significant difference was found in comfort level with active surveillance between pre- and post-video. Post-video, significantly more patients were concerned with frequent doctor visits and making the wrong decision. No significant difference in comfort level was noted between older (>=75) versus younger (<75) respondents.

Canadian Scleroderma Research Group coho.metric: Association of occupational exposures with disease manifestations in Systemic Sclerosis (SSc)
Canadian Scleroderma Research Group coho.notes: Occupational SSc was more prevalent in males. Silica exposure associated with diffuse SSc and mortality. Organic solvents associated with renal disease. Asbestos associated with mortality. Industrial fumes and heavy metal exposure associated with interstitial lung disease (ILD), renal disease, and mortality. Epoxy exposure associated with ILD, renal disease, diffuse SSc, and anti-RNA polymerase III antibody positivity. Lower frequency of anti-centromere antibody noticed in patients exposed to silica, heavy metal, or industrial fumes.

Constructed multimodal skin lesion datas.metric: Average Accuracy of skin lesion classification
Constructed multimodal skin lesion datas.notes: The proposed hierarchical attention fusion network achieved higher accuracy than the simple late-fusion method. The dataset contained 1907 sets of multimodal image pairs covering basal cell carcinoma, melanoma, nevus, squamous cell carcinoma, and seborrheic keratosis.

Melanoma biological drug treatment cohor.metric: Adverse Drug Reactions (ADRs) in melanoma patients treated with biological drugs
Melanoma biological drug treatment cohor.notes: Melanoma patients treated with Nivolumab and Pembrolizumab globally represent 42.20% of treated patients. Serious reactions were more prevalent in Nivolumab (62.50%) compared to Pembrolizumab (33.33%).

Stage I cutaneous melanoma 31-GEP cohort.metric: 5-year recurrence-free survival (RFS) and prognostic value of 31-GEP
Stage I cutaneous melanoma 31-GEP cohort.notes: Class 2B GEP result was an independent predictor of recurrence. The 31-GEP test stratifies 5-year metastatic risk with a higher NPV than SLNBx in the negative SLNBx subset. Most stage I patients have a favorable 5-year prognosis, but 31-GEP can identify those who may benefit from increased surveillance.

architecture_details.key_component: Attention fusion block to learn refined single-modality features through attention information in the complementary imaging modality
architecture_details.fusion_strategy: Hierarchical integration of extracted single-modality features in each stage of the network

training_details.dataset_description: Constructed dataset of 1907 sets of multimodal image pairs (dermoscopy and clinical images) collected from Xiangya Hospital of Central South University over the recent 10 years

conclusions: There are substantial sociodemographic disparities in health information technology (HIT) utilization among skin cancer patients, with uninsured patients and those with lower income or education levels being significantly less likely to utilize online scheduling, email communication, and electronic prescription services.
Targeted interventions are needed to increase HIT utilization among disadvantaged groups to reduce health disparities related to skin cancer.
Most patients are comfortable with active surveillance for basal cell carcinoma (BCC), and providing education on BCCs may alleviate patients' concerns regarding this management strategy.

Educational videos significantly reduced patient concern about doctor monitoring for BCC active surveillance but significantly increased concerns about frequent doctor visits and making the wrong decision.

Occupational exposures (organic solvents, industrial fumes, silica, heavy metals, asbestos, and epoxy resins) are associated with more severe disease phenotypes and increased mortality in systemic sclerosis patients, who are predominantly male.
Silica exposure is associated with diffuse systemic sclerosis and increased mortality; organic solvents with renal disease; and asbestos with increased mortality.

A novel hierarchical attention fusion network for multimodal skin lesion classification achieves higher accuracy (81.2%) compared to simple late-fusion methods (77.3%) by learning refined single-modality features through attention information.
The 31-gene expression profiling (31-GEP) test stratifies 5-year metastatic risk in stage I cutaneous melanoma patients, with Class 2B results being an independent predictor of recurrence (HR 5.8).
The 31-GEP test demonstrates a higher negative predictive value (NPV) for recurrence than sentinel lymph node biopsy (SLNBx) in patients with negative SLNBx results (95% vs 91%).
While most stage I melanoma patients have a favorable 5-year prognosis, 31-GEP can identify those who may benefit from increased surveillance.
important_remarks: The paper is a collection of distinct abstracts from the Journal of Investigative Dermatology (2021), not a single cohesive study, requiring careful separation of findings across different populations and methodologies.

The health information technology (HIT) utilization study relies on retrospective cross-sectional data from the National Health Interview Survey (NHIS) from 2011-2018, which may limit causal inference regarding sociodemographic disparities.

The multimodal skin lesion classification study uses a constructed dataset of only 1907 image pairs from a single institution (Xiangya Hospital), raising concerns about external validity and potential selection bias.
The proposed hierarchical attention fusion network achieved an average accuracy of 81.2% on the constructed dataset, but no external validation on independent datasets was reported.
The systemic sclerosis (SSc) occupational exposure study is limited to a Canadian cohort (n=1525), potentially limiting generalizability to other geographic or occupational contexts.

The adverse drug reaction (ADR) study for melanoma biological drugs is a small retrospective analysis (n=263) with a low number of reported ADRs (n=43), limiting statistical power for comparing Nivolumab and Pembrolizumab.

The 31-GEP prognostic study for stage I cutaneous melanoma (n=850) provides strong prognostic stratification (HR 5.8 for Class 2B), but the subset analysis for negative sentinel lymph node biopsy (SLNBx) results is based on only 325 patients.
The BCC active surveillance survey (n=203) was conducted at a single VA medical center, which may introduce site-specific bias in patient attitudes and concerns.

Several abstracts report odds ratios (OR) and hazard ratios (HR) with confidence intervals (CI) or p-values, but specific confidence intervals for some metrics (e.g., ADR rates, GEP class distributions) are not fully detailed in the provided text.
